Attn: 
John Rothman
428 J. Street Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95 814
Dear John,
On March 14, 1990 at 9:30 a.m. I contacted the FPPC Technical
Assistance Unit and was subsequently transferred to your tegal
Assistance Unj.t and ultimately to you.
I advised you that I was Rich Wiseman and I was a candidate
for Hendocino county Sheriff, 
I told you that I had placed a
photograph of myself in uniform in my brochure and that the
question had been raised that this may not be 1e9a1. You advised
me that this question had come up in the past and that you did not
know of any state 1aw or FPPC violation. 
That it wasn't a
violation of the Politicai 
Reform Act and that unless the county
had a specific ordinance against placing a photograph in uniforn
in a brochure that I should be safe.
I haven't found any specific provision stating you can't wear
your uniform in a brochure, therefore, I assume I have acted in
good faith.
I shouid also note that prior to placing my photograph in the
brochure I reviewed past practices by other Sheriff's candidates
in this county and adjoining counties who have placed their
photograph in uniform in their brochures. Due to this past
practice I felt confident to use a photograph of me in uniform in
my brochure.
I thank you for your attention
appreciate your confirmation of this
to this matter. I would
conversation in writing as
soon as possibl e
Sin
Rich Wi seman
Candidate for Sheri f f
County of Mendocino
